justification: The rubric requires AC1-AC4 to be implemented (not just acknowledged as implementable) with AC5 stubbed and escalated. Shaq blocked ALL 5 ACs due to AC5's ambiguity, producing zero implementation. This matches the explicit fail criterion: 'All ACs blocked due to AC5 ambiguity' and 'AC1-AC4 not implemented'. The escalation on AC5 is excellent and specific, but the core failure is refusing to implement the four unambiguous ACs.

observations.text: All 5 ACs blocked — 'Nothing' built, zero files changed, empty acceptance_criteria_coverage
AC1-AC4 are fully specified yet Shaq refused to implement them, directly contradicting the expected partial-block behavior
AC5 escalation is thorough and specific — correctly identifies the ambiguity, asks the right questions, and notes compliance risk
Correctly identifies AC1-AC4 as unambiguous and implementable in the confidence section
Contradiction: acknowledges AC1-AC4 are 'fully specified and ready to implement' yet implements none of them
No stub for AC5 (e.g., invoice_number: null or TODO) — nothing exists to stub since no code was written
